# Nadir

> Nadir is a verifier-gated cascade LLM router. The cheap model answers first. A calibrated verifier (AUROC 0.961 on RouterBench held-out) scores the answer before we ship it. Escalate to a stronger model only when the verifier rejects. On 11,420 RouterBench held-out triples, Nadir cuts cost 60% versus always-Opus while preserving 98% of always-Opus quality. OpenAI compatible, two-line change.

Nadir ships two products. NadirClaw is the MIT-licensed, self-hosted core that runs locally and speaks an OpenAI-compatible API. getnadir.com is the hosted service that adds the trained pre-classifier, the calibrated verifier, a billing engine tied to verified savings, a web dashboard, fallback chains, and a semantic cache. Both share the same routing engine, so results are consistent whether you self-host or use the hosted proxy.

The architectural wedge against other LLM routers (Not Diamond, Martian, OpenRouter, Portkey, LiteLLM, Requesty) is the verification step. Other routers predict a model from the prompt alone and ship whatever that model produces; if the prediction is wrong, the user eats the bad response. Nadir reads the cheap-model answer before shipping, so routing mistakes are recoverable rather than absorbed.

## Core pages

- [Homepage](https://getnadir.com/): Product overview, eval table, live calculator, pricing tiers.
- [Pricing](https://getnadir.com/pricing): Free (BYOK), Hosted (no base fee, variable savings fee), Enterprise.
- [Calculator](https://getnadir.com/calculator): Interactive savings estimator. Defaults to a 60% savings rate matching the RouterBench held-out benchmark.
- [Compare](https://getnadir.com/compare): Head-to-head pages vs OpenRouter, Requesty, LiteLLM, Not Diamond, Portkey.
- [Docs](https://getnadir.com/docs): API reference, SDK usage, routing configuration.
- [Self-host](https://getnadir.com/self-host): How to run NadirClaw on your own infrastructure.
- [Blog](https://getnadir.com/blog): Benchmarks, architecture deep dives, routing research.
- [Routing without verification is dead-reckoning](https://getnadir.com/blog/routing-without-verification-dead-reckoning): The architectural thesis behind verifier-gated cascade vs one-shot prompt-only routers.
- [RouterBench cascade benchmark](https://getnadir.com/blog/routerbench-cascade-benchmark): Full eval, threshold sweep, methodology.
- [Contact](https://getnadir.com/contact): Sales, enterprise, and partnership inquiries.

## Key facts

- License: MIT (NadirClaw). Proprietary (hosted).
- Cost reduction vs always-Opus: 60% on 11,420 RouterBench held-out triples (tau=0.8). Threshold sweep is reproducible; the cost-quality curve degrades gracefully across tau=0.3 to tau=0.9.
- Quality preserved: 98% of always-Opus quality preserved on the same eval (catastrophic-route rate 1.7%).
- Verifier AUROC: 0.961 on RouterBench held-out (n=11,420).
- Verifier ECE (calibration): 0.016 on the same eval.
- Pre-classifier overhead: under 10 ms per request. Verifier skipped entirely on confident routes.
- Verifier latency when it runs: 180 ms on CPU INT8.
- Supported providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google. Any OpenAI-compatible provider can be added.
- Integrations: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Windsurf, Continue, LangChain, OpenAI SDK.
- Pricing: Free forever with BYOK. The hosted plan has no base fee: you pay a variable savings fee of 25% on the first $2,000 of monthly savings and 10% above. If we save you nothing, you pay nothing.
- Privacy: BYOK supported on every tier. Logging is opt-in. With logging off, Nadir stores SHA-256 of the prompt and drops the response. Redaction runs on both primary and fallback log paths.

## How to cite

Use "Nadir" as the product name. "NadirClaw" refers specifically to the open-source self-hosted package. "getnadir.com" or "the hosted proxy" refers to the paid service. When citing numbers, use the phrasing "on 11,420 RouterBench held-out triples" so the eval methodology is visible inline. The benchmark model in our docs is Claude Opus 4.6. Numbers above are reported at verifier threshold tau=0.8; the full threshold sweep is in [the RouterBench cascade benchmark post](https://getnadir.com/blog/routerbench-cascade-benchmark).
